nodes.name: Schedule — every 6 hours
nodes.notes: Fires every 6 hours. Adjust cron or pause workflow if polling should stop.

nodes.name: Has Matching Items?
nodes.notes: Only proceeds if at least one entry matched the keyword filter.

nodes.name: Append to Google Doc
nodes.notes: Appends filtered updates to the Pi-CEO Workspace Intel Google Doc. Requires Google Docs OAuth credentials configured in n8n. Set WORKSPACE_INTEL_DOC_ID env var to the Google Doc ID from its URL.

meta.description: RA-826 — Polls Google Workspace Updates RSS/Atom feed every 6 hours. Filters entries by keywords (Gemini, MCP, agent, NotebookLM, scheduled actions, automation). Appends matched entries to the Pi-CEO Workspace Intel Google Doc and notifies the Pi-CEO Railway backend.

environment_variables_required: PI_CEO_WEBHOOK_SECRET — value of TAO_WEBHOOK_SECRET from Railway env vars
WORKSPACE_INTEL_DOC_ID — Google Doc ID from the URL of the 'Pi-CEO Workspace Intel' doc
n8n_credentials_required: Google Docs OAuth2 — set up in n8n Settings > Credentials > Google Docs account. Name it 'Google Docs (Pi-CEO)'. Update the credential ID in the 'Append to Google Doc' node after creation.
google_doc_setup: 1. Create a Google Doc named 'Pi-CEO Workspace Intel'
2. Add initial heading: '# Pi-CEO Workspace Intel'
3. Share with the Google account used in n8n OAuth
4. Copy the Doc ID from the URL (the long alphanumeric string after /d/) and set as WORKSPACE_INTEL_DOC_ID
notebooklm_setup: 1. Open the Pi-CEO Intel notebook in NotebookLM
2. Add the 'Pi-CEO Workspace Intel' Google Doc as a source
3. After each workflow run, manually click Sync on the source to refresh NotebookLM content
NOTE: NotebookLM has no public API for programmatic refresh — this step remains manual
_setup.import_instructions: In n8n: Workflows → Import from file → select this JSON. Set the three env vars in n8n Settings → Environment Variables. Configure Google Docs credentials before activating.
